title: phegopteris pavoniana
intro: Phegopteris pavoniana, also known as the peacock fern, is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. This plant is traditionally used in herbal medicine, with its medicinal properties attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Phegopteris pavoniana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and exhibit antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of Phegopteris pavoniana typically involve drying and processing the plant material into teas, infusions, or capsules, which are then used to harness its medicinal properties.
